note: rendered from the sandbox via the public pollinations endpoint to test second-tier prompt behavior; production renders use render-fipo-posters.yml (gpt-image-1). Prompt shown is v2; the test render used v1 (pre-glyph-guardrail).

causal.label: the title came first
causal.blurb: exhibitor-tested before a frame existed; the picture was commissioned to deserve it

distance.label: recognizably descended
distance.blurb: the lineage is visible to anyone who has seen a movie

novum.premise: water is the only currency left, and the ice must be pirated
novum.short: water is money and the ice must be stolen

star.label: the entire cast of a cancelled sitcom

dramatis.sidekick: a hologram of the dead mentor, buffering

threat.label: an alien empire that finds Earth unimpressive

commitment.text: The hero’s weapon is a lamp. It is always a lamp.

era.label: the Cannon / VHS-box decade
era.anxiety: nukes again, but really: Star Wars money

milieu.label: a Corman-style New World shop
milieu.blurb: fast, cheap, and weirdly proud of it; everybody here will be famous later or never

budget.label: exploitation-standard
budget.blurb: enough for the monster, once, from behind

comps.line: The Wizard of Oz meets Spartacus — in a single soundstage.

title.text: I Was a Two-Headed Ice Pirate

genome.tagline: Where single soundstage meets the void.
genome.logline: A park ranger must expose an alien empire that finds Earth unimpressive when water is the only currency left, and the ice must be pirated — or the galaxy learns the price.

failures.label: scale misjudgment
failures.reason: The fate of the galaxy will be decided in a single soundstage.

failures.label: miscasting
failures.reason: The entire cast of a cancelled sitcom as a park ranger: the casting call is the special effect.

failures.label: failed transcription
failures.reason: It has seen The Wizard of Oz, and remembers it fondly, incorrectly. What got lost: whatever made it work.

failures.label: budget hallucination
failures.reason: The poster will promise the galaxy; the budget reaches a single soundstage.

failures.label: trope collision
failures.reason: The Wizard of Oz meets Spartacus — in a single soundstage. The collision is the content.

brief.text: pirates boarding an ice freighter under the aurora on a sparse theatrical set, with a fleet of silver disc-ships

composition.text: Composition: an ensemble pyramid: the star largest at the apex, the villain opposite, the supporting cast descending in size

medium.text: A 1985 American science fiction film poster, photochemical one-sheet, airbrushed gouache on board, rich saturated color, painted lens flare

flaws.text: the heroine’s hands are slightly too small
the monster has two rows of teeth too many, painted with great care

projection.prompt:
A 1985 American science fiction film poster, photochemical one-sheet, airbrushed gouache on board, rich saturated color, painted lens flare.
Composition: an ensemble pyramid: the star largest at the apex, the villain opposite, the supporting cast descending in size.
Subject: pirates boarding an ice freighter under the aurora on a sparse theatrical set, with a fleet of silver disc-ships.
Painted details: the heroine’s hands are slightly too small; the monster has two rows of teeth too many, painted with great care.
Rich saturated color. The top and bottom fifths of the painting stay simple, dark, and uncluttered — space reserved for lettering. No text, no lettering, no logo, no signature, no glyphs, no letterforms of any kind.
Painted entirely by hand in the period style; not a photograph, not a digital montage.

typography.title: I Was a Two-Headed Ice Pirate
typography.tagline: Where single soundstage meets the void.

renderedPrompt:
A 1985 American science fiction film poster, photochemical one-sheet, airbrushed gouache on board, rich saturated color, painted lens flare.
Composition: an ensemble pyramid: the star largest at the apex, the villain opposite, the supporting cast descending in size.
Subject: pirates boarding an ice freighter under the aurora on a sparse theatrical set, with a fleet of silver disc-ships.
Painted details: the heroine’s hands are slightly too small; the monster has two rows of teeth too many, painted with great care.
Rich saturated color, space reserved at the top and bottom for lettering. No text, no lettering, no logo.
Painted entirely by hand in the period style; not a photograph, not a digital montage.
